title: Solenospermum littorale
intro: Solenospermum littorale, also known as the coastal Solenospermum, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in treating various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Solenospermum littorale include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Solenospermum littorale include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.

This family comprises over 5,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Solenospermum is a small group of plants, and Solenospermum littorale is the only species in this genus that is commonly recognized. Solenospermum littorale is native to the tropical regions of Australia.
